description: Cross-sector baseline compliance controls for France v1. Rules are evidence-driven and deterministic; absence of evidence does not by itself constitute legal non-compliance.

metadata.scope: France-only baseline controls for document/document-set review.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official French/EU legal, regulatory, supervisory, and guidance sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR/RGPD) and CNIL professional guidance
CNIL guidance on information and transparency
ANSSI cybersecurity guidance and hygiene materials
DGCCRF consumer protection and market-practice materials

rules.title: Privacy notice or personal-data transparency evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document contains a privacy notice, transparency disclosure, privacy policy, or equivalent RGPD information.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Personal-data purpose and lawful-basis evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document describes processing purposes and a lawful basis or consent/contract/legal-obligation basis.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Data-subject rights and contact channel evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks whether the document explains rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection, portability, or a DPO/contact channel.

rules.title: Cybersecurity governance or security-control evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for security policy, access control, incident response, backup, logging, encryption, or cyber-risk management evidence.

rules.title: Consumer information, pricing, or complaints evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for consumer disclosures, pricing, terms of sale, claims accuracy, complaints, withdrawal rights, or redress routes.

rules.title: AML/CFT screening or suspicious-reporting route is evidenced where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for customer due diligence, beneficial ownership, sanctions screening, suspicious transaction reporting, or TRACFIN escalation evidence.
